questions.q: Kisan Kanya is remembered in Indian cinema history for being the first Indian-made film of what kind?
options: The first Indian colour feature film
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya (1937) was the first Indian-made colour feature film.

questions.q: In which year was the film Kisan Kanya released?
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya was released in 1937.

questions.q: Which film company produced Kisan Kanya?
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya was produced by Ardeshir Irani's Imperial Film Company.

questions.q: Which pioneering producer was behind the making of Kisan Kanya?
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya was produced by Ardeshir Irani, founder of the Imperial Film Company.

questions.q: Who directed the film Kisan Kanya?
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya was directed by Moti B. Gidvani.

questions.q: Kisan Kanya was made in which primary language?
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya was made in Hindi-Urdu (Hindustani).

questions.q: The title 'Kisan Kanya' translates roughly to which of the following in English?
questions.explanation: 'Kisan Kanya' means 'The Farmer's Daughter' (or 'Peasant Girl').

questions.q: Ardeshir Irani, the producer of Kisan Kanya, was earlier famous for producing which landmark Indian film of 1931?
questions.explanation: Ardeshir Irani had earlier produced Alam Ara (1931), India's first sound film.

questions.q: In which city was the Imperial Film Company, the producer of Kisan Kanya, based?
questions.explanation: The Imperial Film Company was based in Bombay (now Mumbai).

questions.q: Kisan Kanya was a feature film that broadly dealt with the hardships of which section of Indian society?
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya focused on the lives and hardships of farmers and peasants.

questions.q: Which colour processing system was used to make Kisan Kanya, India's first colour feature?
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya was processed using the Cinecolor system.

questions.q: How many years after India's first sound film Alam Ara (1931) was Kisan Kanya released?
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya (1937) was released six years after Alam Ara (1931).

questions.q: The Cinecolor process used to make Kisan Kanya was a colour technology that originated in which country?
questions.explanation: Cinecolor was an American colour process; Ardeshir Irani brought the technology to India for Kisan Kanya.

questions.q: Which of these statements about Kisan Kanya is correct?
options: It was India's first silent feature
It was the first Indian film shot abroad
It was the first Indian-made colour feature film
It was the first Indian animated film
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya holds the distinction of being the first Indian-made colour feature film.

questions.q: The earlier 1931 film by Kisan Kanya's producer, Alam Ara, is significant in Indian cinema for being the first to feature what?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara (1931) was India's first sound film, featuring synchronised dialogue and songs.

questions.q: In the decade Kisan Kanya was released, the dominant film format in India was overwhelmingly which of these?
questions.explanation: Most Indian films of the 1930s were black-and-white, making Kisan Kanya's colour a rarity.

questions.q: Kisan Kanya is best described as belonging to which category of film artifact in Indian cinema history?
options: A foreign import dubbed into Hindi
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya is celebrated as a pioneering technical 'first' as India's debut colour feature.

questions.q: Which of the following correctly pairs a film with its 'first' in Indian cinema?
options: Kisan Kanya — first sound film; Alam Ara — first colour film
Kisan Kanya — first silent film; Alam Ara — first colour film
Kisan Kanya — first colour feature; Alam Ara — first sound film
Kisan Kanya — first 3D film; Alam Ara — first silent film
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya was the first colour feature and Alam Ara was the first sound film, both produced by Ardeshir Irani.

questions.q: Moti B. Gidvani, the director of Kisan Kanya, primarily worked in which industry of his era?
questions.explanation: Moti B. Gidvani was an Indian film director active in Bombay's film industry.

questions.q: Roughly which decade saw the release of Kisan Kanya, India's first colour feature?
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya was released in 1937, in the 1930s.

questions.q: Kisan Kanya followed which earlier milestone by the same producer in terms of technical innovation?
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya followed Ardeshir Irani's earlier landmark Alam Ara, India's first sound film.

questions.q: The Cinecolor process used for Kisan Kanya was fundamentally a colour system that reproduced images using how many primary colour components?
questions.explanation: Cinecolor was a two-colour (bipack) subtractive process used to process Kisan Kanya.

questions.q: Ardeshir Irani's company that produced Kisan Kanya, the Imperial Film Company, was founded in which approximate period?
questions.explanation: Ardeshir Irani founded the Imperial Film Company in the mid-1920s (1926).

questions.q: Despite being India's first colour feature, Kisan Kanya is generally regarded by historians as having achieved what at the box office?
options: The highest grosser of the decade
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya is remembered chiefly as a technical milestone rather than a major commercial success.

questions.q: What was the chief reason Kisan Kanya is principally remembered in Indian film history, rather than for its story or stars?
options: It was the first Indian film with sound
It introduced colour to Indian feature filmmaking
It was India's first international co-production
It was the first film banned by Indian censors
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya is remembered primarily as a technical landmark for introducing colour to Indian feature filmmaking, rather than for its narrative or cast.

questions.q: Within Ardeshir Irani's two famous 'firsts', where does Kisan Kanya fall chronologically relative to Alam Ara?
options: It was made before Alam Ara
It was made the same year as Alam Ara
It was made several years after Alam Ara
It was made after Indian independence
questions.explanation: Alam Ara appeared in 1931 and Kisan Kanya in 1937, so Kisan Kanya came several years after Alam Ara, and both predate independence (1947).

questions.q: Which statement about the colour technology of Kisan Kanya is most accurate?
options: It used the same three-strip Technicolor as Hollywood epics
It was hand-painted frame by frame
It used the imported two-colour Cinecolor process
questions.explanation: Kisan Kanya used the imported two-colour Cinecolor process, not Hollywood's three-strip Technicolor.

questions.q: Ardeshir Irani's pioneering work on both Alam Ara and Kisan Kanya places him most prominently as a pioneer of which aspect of Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Irani is celebrated as a pioneer of technical innovation, introducing both sound (Alam Ara) and colour (Kisan Kanya) to Indian cinema.

questions.q: After Kisan Kanya's 1937 debut, full colour filmmaking only became common in mainstream Indian cinema in which later decade?
questions.explanation: Despite Kisan Kanya's 1937 first, colour filmmaking became widespread in Indian cinema only from the 1950s and 1960s.

questions.q: Which pair of Indian cinema 'firsts' is correctly attributed to producer Ardeshir Irani?
options: First silent film and first 3D film
First talkie and first colour feature
First English film and first regional film
questions.explanation: Ardeshir Irani produced India's first talkie (Alam Ara, 1931) and first colour feature (Kisan Kanya, 1937).
